title: The Mussar Movement: Rabbi Yisrael Salanter's Revolution
title_zh: 穆萨尔运动:拉比伊斯雷尔·萨兰特的革命
title_he: תנועת המוסר: המהפכה של רבי ישראל סלנטר
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
summary: The story of Rabbi Yisrael Salanter's revolutionary approach to ethical self-improvement in 19th-century Lithuania, exploring the systematic methods of the Mussar Movement and drawing parallels to Confucian self-cultivation traditions.
cultural_parallels: Chinese practice of self-reflection (内省, neixin)
Comparison between 君子 (junzi) ideal and Jewish ethical development
target_audience: Chinese Mandarin speakers interested in Jewish ethics and character development, those exploring comparative philosophy
learning_outcomes: Understanding the origins and goals of the Mussar Movement
Learning Rabbi Yisrael Salanter's systematic approach to character development
Recognizing parallels between Jewish and Confucian ethical traditions
Practical methods for implementing mussar practice in daily life
Historical context of 19th-century Lithuanian Jewish life
discussion_questions: How does the Mussar Movement's approach to character development compare to modern self-help methods?
What are the similarities and differences between Jewish mussar and Confucian self-cultivation?
Why did Rabbi Salanter believe that systematic ethical study was necessary even for Torah scholars?
How can the practice of cheshbon hanefesh (self-examination) be integrated into modern life?
What makes the 'one trait at a time' approach effective for character development?
